title: cerastium macrocarpum subsp. lucorum
intro: Cerastium macrocarpum subsp. lucorum, also known as the high-altitude mouse-ear chickweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant that belongs to the carnation family. Traditionally, this plant has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Cerastium macrocarpum subsp. lucorum include its potential to reduce swelling and alleviate symptoms of respiratory tract infections, as well as its ability to soothe digestive issues. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
